articles.headline: Parliament Passes Landmark Urban Local Bodies Empowerment Bill, 2024
articles.explainer:
• The Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha passed the Urban Local Bodies Empowerment Bill, 2024, aiming to strengthen decentralised governance.
• The Bill proposes greater financial autonomy for ULBs, direct devolution of funds, and enhanced capacity building programs.
• It mandates the establishment of Ward Committees in all municipalities and introduces participatory budgeting mechanisms.
• Key provisions include a performance-linked grant system and a dedicated urban infrastructure development fund.
• The legislation is expected to significantly boost the effectiveness and accountability of urban local self-governments.
gsSubTopics: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States
issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure
devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein
prelimsPoints: Urban Local Bodies Empowerment Bill, 2024
Aims to strengthen 74th Constitutional Amendment Act provisions
Provisions for financial autonomy, direct fund devolution, capacity building
Mandates Ward Committees and participatory budgeting
Performance-linked grants and urban infrastructure fund
mainsPoints: Significance of the Bill in reinforcing democratic decentralisation in urban areas.
Challenges in implementation, including financial constraints and capacity deficits of ULBs.
Role of participatory budgeting and Ward Committees in enhancing citizen engagement and accountability.
Impact on urban planning, service delivery, and sustainable development goals.
Comparison with previous attempts at ULB reforms and lessons learned.
articles.imageDescription: Indian Parliament building with 'Urban Local Bodies Empowerment Bill' text overlay
articles.trivia: The 74th Constitutional Amendment Act, which gave constitutional status to Urban Local Bodies, was passed in 1992.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate at 6.5%, Focuses on Inflation Moderation
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) unanimously decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5% for the sixth consecutive review.
• The decision comes amidst ongoing efforts to bring inflation within the target band, despite some moderation in recent months.
• RBI Governor highlighted global economic uncertainties and volatile crude oil prices as key risks to the inflation outlook.
• The MPC reiterated its commitment to 'withdrawal of accommodation' to ensure inflation aligns with the target while supporting growth.
• Analysts anticipate a cautious approach from the RBI, with potential rate cuts deferred until early next year.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning
mobilization of resources
growth
development and employment
Government Budgeting
Monetary Policy
prelimsPoints: Repo rate maintained at 6.5%
Sixth consecutive review without change
MPC's stance: 'withdrawal of accommodation'
Inflation remains a key concern for RBI
Global economic uncertainties cited as risk factors
mainsPoints: Analyze the rationale behind RBI's decision to maintain the repo rate in the current economic scenario.
Discuss the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control and economic growth.
Evaluate the effectiveness of 'withdrawal of accommodation' as a monetary policy stance.
Examine the potential impact of global economic factors, such as crude oil prices, on India's inflation trajectory.
Suggest alternative measures or policy tools RBI could employ to achieve its objectives.
articles.imageDescription: Reserve Bank of India headquarters building in Mumbai
articles.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) in India has six members – three officials from the RBI and three external experts nominated by the Government of India.

articles.headline: India-EU Strategic Dialogue Concludes, Emphasizes Green Transition and Trade
articles.explainer:
• The 18th India-EU Strategic Dialogue concluded in Brussels, focusing on deepening cooperation in green technologies, digital transformation, and trade.
• External Affairs Minister and EU High Representative for Foreign Affairs co-chaired the dialogue, reviewing progress on the India-EU Free Trade Agreement negotiations.
• Both sides committed to enhancing collaboration on climate action, renewable energy, and sustainable development goals.
• Discussions also covered geopolitical developments, regional security, and strengthening multilateralism.
• A joint statement highlighted the shared commitment to rules-based international order and resilient supply chains.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India's interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India's interests
prelimsPoints: 18th India-EU Strategic Dialogue in Brussels
Co-chaired by EAM and EU High Representative
Key focus areas: green technologies, digital transformation, trade
Progress reviewed on India-EU FTA negotiations
Commitment to climate action and sustainable development
mainsPoints: Analyze the growing strategic convergence between India and the EU, particularly in the context of global geopolitical shifts.
Discuss the potential economic benefits and challenges of the proposed India-EU Free Trade Agreement.
Examine the role of green transition and digital cooperation as pillars of the India-EU partnership.
Evaluate India's position and influence within multilateral forums through its engagement with the EU.
Suggest measures to further deepen the India-EU strategic partnership beyond economic and climate cooperation.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and European Union displayed together during a press conference
articles.trivia: The European Union is India's third-largest trading partner, accounting for 10.8% of India's total trade in goods in 2022-23.

articles.headline: Government Unveils National Policy on Water Recycling and Reuse, 2024
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Jal Shakti launched the National Policy on Water Recycling and Reuse, 2024, aiming to promote sustainable water management.
• The policy sets targets for increasing the share of treated wastewater in non-potable uses across urban, industrial, and agricultural sectors.
• It emphasizes the development of robust infrastructure for wastewater treatment and distribution, along with innovative financing mechanisms.
• Key objectives include reducing freshwater demand, enhancing water security, and mitigating water pollution.
• The policy encourages public-private partnerships and awareness campaigns for behavioral change.
gsSubTopics: Conservation
environmental pollution and degradation
environmental impact assessment
Water Resources
prelimsPoints: National Policy on Water Recycling and Reuse, 2024
Launched by Ministry of Jal Shakti
Aims to increase treated wastewater use for non-potable purposes
Focus on urban, industrial, and agricultural sectors
Promotes infrastructure development, financing, PPPs, and awareness
mainsPoints: Discuss the critical need for a national policy on water recycling and reuse in India, considering its water scarcity challenges.
Evaluate the policy's potential impact on freshwater demand, water security, and pollution control.
Analyze the challenges in implementing the policy, including infrastructure gaps, technological limitations, and public perception.
Examine the role of different stakeholders (government, industry, local bodies, citizens) in achieving the policy objectives.
Suggest innovative approaches and best practices from other countries that India can adopt for effective water reuse.
articles.imageDescription: Graphic showing water recycling process with arrows indicating reuse in different sectors
articles.trivia: It is estimated that by 2050, global demand for water will increase by 55%, primarily due to manufacturing, electricity generation, and domestic use.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Advanced Earth Observation Satellite EOS-07
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully launched its advanced Earth Observation Satellite (EOS-07) from Satish Dhawan Space Centre.
• EOS-07, carried by the PSLV-C59 rocket, is designed to provide high-resolution imagery for various applications, including urban planning, agriculture, and disaster management.
• This mission also carried two smaller co-passenger satellites, demonstrating ISRO's capability for multi-satellite launches.
• The satellite features enhanced spectral bands and improved spatial resolution, promising more precise data for environmental monitoring and resource mapping.
• The launch reinforces India's growing capabilities in space technology and its commitment to utilizing space for national development.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology
indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Space Technology
prelimsPoints: ISRO launches EOS-07
Launched from Satish Dhawan Space Centre
Carried by PSLV-C59 rocket
EOS-07 for high-resolution imagery (urban planning, agriculture, disaster management)
Co-passenger satellites also launched
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of Earth Observation Satellites like EOS-07 for India's socio-economic development and strategic interests.
Analyze how advanced remote sensing capabilities contribute to areas like disaster management, agricultural productivity, and environmental monitoring.
Evaluate ISRO's role in developing indigenous space technology and promoting self-reliance in the space sector.
Examine the challenges and opportunities for India in the global space industry, including commercial launches and international collaborations.
Suggest future directions for India's Earth observation program to address emerging national needs and global challenges.
articles.imageDescription: Rocket taking off from launchpad with 'ISRO' logo visible
articles.trivia: India's first experimental satellite, Aryabhata, was launched by the Soviet Union in 1975.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Directs States to Expedite Implementation of Police Reforms
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, hearing a Public Interest Litigation (PIL), issued fresh directives to all state governments and Union Territories for the expeditious implementation of police reforms.
• The court expressed concern over the slow progress in adopting recommendations from the Prakash Singh case and various expert committees.
• Directives include setting up State Security Commissions, ensuring fixed tenure for DGPs, and separating investigation from law and order functions.
• The bench emphasized the need for modernizing police infrastructure, improving training, and enhancing accountability mechanisms.
• This ruling reiterates the judiciary's push for a more professional, accountable, and citizen-friendly police force.
gsSubTopics: Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary
Ministries and Departments of the Government
pressure groups and formal/informal associations and their role in the Polity
Governance
transparency and accountability
keyTerms: Prakash Singh v. Union of India
Separation of Investigation and Law & Order
prelimsPoints: Supreme Court directs states on police reforms
References Prakash Singh case recommendations
Key directives: State Security Commissions, fixed DGP tenure, separation of investigation
Emphasizes modernization, training, accountability
Aims for professional and citizen-friendly police force
mainsPoints: Analyze the historical context and persistent challenges in implementing police reforms in India.
Discuss the key recommendations of the Prakash Singh case and their significance for police accountability and efficiency.
Evaluate the role of the judiciary in pushing for police reforms when the executive and legislature have shown slow progress.
Examine the potential impact of fixed tenures for DGPs and the separation of investigation functions on police professionalism.
Suggest comprehensive strategies, including legislative changes and public awareness, to achieve effective police reforms.
articles.imageDescription: Supreme Court of India building facade
articles.trivia: The 'Prakash Singh v. Union of India' judgment was delivered by the Supreme Court in 2006, outlining seven directives for police reforms.

articles.headline: Ministry of Electronics Releases Draft Cyber Security Framework 2.0 for Public Consultation
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) has released the draft National Cyber Security Framework 2.0 for public feedback.
• The updated framework aims to address evolving cyber threats, including those posed by AI, IoT, and quantum computing.
• Key pillars include robust critical infrastructure protection, enhanced cyber deterrence, and fostering a culture of cyber hygiene.
• It proposes a multi-stakeholder approach involving government, industry, academia, and civil society for effective implementation.
• The framework emphasizes capacity building, international cooperation, and a legal structure to combat cybercrime more effectively.
gsSubTopics: Challenges to internal security through communication networks
role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges
basics of cyber security
money-laundering and its prevention
articles.source: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology
prelimsPoints: Draft National Cyber Security Framework 2.0 released by MeitY
Aims to counter AI, IoT, quantum computing threats
Pillars: critical infrastructure protection, cyber deterrence, cyber hygiene
Proposes multi-stakeholder approach
Emphasizes capacity building, international cooperation, legal structure
mainsPoints: Discuss the increasing complexity of cyber threats in the digital age and the necessity for an updated cyber security framework.
Analyze the key provisions of the National Cyber Security Framework 2.0 and its potential effectiveness in protecting India's digital assets.
Evaluate the challenges in implementing such a comprehensive framework, including technological gaps, human resource shortages, and inter-agency coordination.
Examine the role of international cooperation and data governance in strengthening India's cyber defenses.
Suggest measures to foster a strong culture of cyber hygiene among individuals and organizations in India.
articles.imageDescription: Abstract digital network lines representing cybersecurity
articles.trivia: India established the Computer Emergency Response Team - India (CERT-In) in 2004 as the national agency for responding to computer security incidents.

articles.headline: National Education Policy (NEP) Implementation Review Highlights Progress and Challenges
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education released a comprehensive review report on the implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
• The report noted significant progress in areas like foundational literacy and numeracy, curricular reforms, and integration of vocational education.
• Challenges identified include infrastructure gaps in remote areas, teacher training effectiveness, and equitable access to digital learning resources.
• The review emphasized the need for greater state-level coordination and increased budgetary allocation for education.
• Recommendations include accelerated teacher recruitment, strengthening digital infrastructure, and developing localized content.
gsSubTopics: Development processes and the development industry—the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
prelimsPoints: Ministry of Education releases NEP 2020 implementation review
Progress in foundational literacy, curricular reforms, vocational education
Challenges: infrastructure, teacher training, digital access
Recommendations: state coordination, budget increase, teacher recruitment
Focus on localized content development
mainsPoints: Analyze the transformative potential of the National Education Policy 2020 in reforming India's education system.
Discuss the key achievements in NEP implementation over the past few years, citing specific examples.
Examine the major hurdles and bottlenecks encountered during the policy's implementation, particularly in rural and underserved areas.
Suggest concrete policy interventions and resource allocation strategies to address the identified challenges and ensure equitable access to quality education.
Evaluate the long-term implications of NEP 2020 for India's human resource development and economic growth.
articles.imageDescription: Students in a classroom setting with digital learning tools
articles.trivia: The National Education Policy 2020 is the third major education policy in India since independence, following those in 1968 and 1986.

articles.headline: Government Announces New PLI Scheme for Advanced Battery Manufacturing
articles.explainer:
• The Union Government announced a new Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme aimed at boosting domestic manufacturing of Advanced Chemistry Cell (ACC) batteries.
• The scheme, with an outlay of ₹X,XXX crore, seeks to attract significant investment and establish gigafactories for battery production.
• It is crucial for India's electric vehicle (EV) mission and energy storage initiatives, reducing reliance on imports.
• The PLI scheme offers incentives based on sales of advanced batteries, promoting both scale and cutting-edge technology.
• This move aligns with India's broader goal of becoming a global manufacturing hub and achieving energy independence.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Investment models
prelimsPoints: New PLI scheme for Advanced Chemistry Cell (ACC) batteries
Aims to boost domestic manufacturing and attract investment
Crucial for India's EV mission and energy storage
Incentives based on sales of advanced batteries
Part of 'Make in India' and energy independence goals
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of domestic Advanced Chemistry Cell battery manufacturing for India's economic and energy security.
Analyze how the new PLI scheme can catalyze investment, technological innovation, and job creation in the battery sector.
Evaluate the challenges in establishing a robust battery manufacturing ecosystem in India, including raw material sourcing and technological expertise.
Examine the role of PLI schemes in promoting 'Aatmanirbhar Bharat' and reducing import dependence across various sectors.
Suggest complementary policy measures required to ensure the long-term success and sustainability of India's EV and energy storage goals.
articles.imageDescription: Graphic illustrating battery cells and EV charging stations
articles.trivia: Lithium-ion batteries are currently the most common type of Advanced Chemistry Cells used in electric vehicles and grid-scale energy storage.

articles.headline: India Advocates for Global South Concerns at UN Climate Conference Prep Meet
articles.explainer:
• India actively participated in the preparatory meeting for the upcoming UN Climate Change Conference (COP29), advocating for the concerns of the Global South.
• The Indian delegation emphasized the principles of Common But Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities (CBDR-RC).
• Key demands included increased climate finance from developed nations, technology transfer, and support for adaptation measures in developing countries.
• India highlighted the need for a balanced approach to climate action, ensuring energy security and sustainable development for all nations.
• The discussions set the stage for contentious negotiations on mitigation, adaptation, and loss and damage at COP29.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India's interests
articles.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
keyTerms: Common But Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities (CBDR-RC)
prelimsPoints: India at UN Climate Conference (COP29) prep meet
Advocated for Global South concerns
Emphasized CBDR-RC principle
Demands: increased climate finance, technology transfer, adaptation support
Called for balanced approach to climate action
mainsPoints: Analyze India's leadership role in representing the interests of the Global South in international climate negotiations.
Discuss the significance of the principle of 'Common But Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities' (CBDR-RC) in achieving climate justice.
Evaluate the challenges in mobilizing adequate climate finance and technology transfer from developed to developing countries.
Examine the implications of climate change on India's development trajectory and its efforts towards energy transition.
Suggest strategies for India to strengthen its position and achieve favorable outcomes for the Global South at COP29.
articles.imageDescription: World map with countries highlighted for climate change discussions
articles.trivia: The first Conference of the Parties (COP) to the UNFCCC was held in Berlin, Germany, in 1995.

articles.headline: Conservation Efforts Show Promising Increase in Snow Leopard Population in Indian Himalayas
articles.explainer:
• A recent survey conducted by the Wildlife Institute of India (WII) and state forest departments indicates a significant increase in the snow leopard population in the Indian Himalayas.
• The survey, part of the 'Snow Leopard Population Assessment in India' (SPAI) initiative, used camera trapping and scat analysis.
• This positive trend is attributed to enhanced conservation efforts, community-based protection programs, and anti-poaching measures.
• The snow leopard (Panthera uncia) is listed as 'Vulnerable' on the IUCN Red List and is a key indicator species for mountain ecosystems.
• The success highlights the importance of transboundary cooperation and sustainable livelihood options for local communities.
gsSubTopics: Conservation
environmental pollution and degradation
environmental impact assessment
Biodiversity
prelimsPoints: Snow leopard population increase in Indian Himalayas
Survey by WII and state forest departments
Part of 'Snow Leopard Population Assessment in India' (SPAI)
Attributed to conservation efforts, community programs, anti-poaching
Snow leopard is 'Vulnerable' on IUCN Red List
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological significance of the snow leopard as an apex predator and an indicator species of the health of high-altitude ecosystems.
Analyze the various conservation strategies employed in the Indian Himalayas that have contributed to the increase in snow leopard numbers.
Examine the challenges in snow leopard conservation, including human-wildlife conflict, habitat fragmentation, and climate change impacts.
Evaluate the role of community participation and transboundary cooperation in effective wildlife protection.
Suggest further measures to ensure the long-term survival and genetic viability of the snow leopard population in India.
articles.imageDescription: Snow leopard walking in a snowy mountainous landscape
articles.trivia: Snow leopards are solitary animals and are incredibly elusive, often referred to as 'ghosts of the mountains'.

articles.headline: Parliamentary Panel Recommends Reforms in Anti-Defection Law to Ensure Stability
articles.explainer:
• A Joint Parliamentary Committee (JPC) submitted its report recommending significant reforms to the Anti-Defection Law (Tenth Schedule of the Constitution).
• The report suggests clarifying the definition of 'defection' and limiting the Speaker's discretionary powers in deciding disqualification petitions.
• Recommendations include setting a strict time limit for the Speaker to decide on defection cases and exploring the role of the Election Commission.
• The panel aimed to prevent frequent defections leading to government instability while safeguarding genuine dissent.
• The proposed changes seek to strike a balance between party discipline and the democratic rights of legislators.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these
prelimsPoints: JPC recommends reforms to Anti-Defection Law (Tenth Schedule)
Suggestions: clarify defection, limit Speaker's powers
Strict time limit for Speaker's decision proposed
Role of Election Commission to be explored
Aims to prevent instability and protect dissent
mainsPoints: Discuss the original intent and evolution of the Anti-Defection Law in India and its impact on political stability.
Analyze the criticisms leveled against the current Anti-Defection Law, particularly regarding the Speaker's role and the ambiguity of 'defection'.
Evaluate the recommendations of the Parliamentary Panel, such as limiting the Speaker's powers and setting time limits, for their potential effectiveness.
Examine the implications of these reforms for party discipline, legislative integrity, and the democratic rights of elected representatives.
Suggest comprehensive reforms to the Anti-Defection Law that can balance political stability with democratic principles.
articles.imageDescription: Indian Parliament building with scales of justice overlay
articles.trivia: The Anti-Defection Law was added to the Indian Constitution as the Tenth Schedule by the 52nd Amendment Act of 1985.

articles.headline: Indian Scientists Announce Breakthrough in Indigenous Quantum Computing Research
articles.explainer:
• Researchers at the Indian Institute of Science (IISc) and a DRDO lab have announced a significant breakthrough in indigenous quantum computing research.
• The team successfully demonstrated a stable 16-qubit quantum processor, a crucial step towards developing fault-tolerant quantum computers.
• This achievement positions India among a select few nations with advanced capabilities in quantum technology development.
• The research has implications for secure communications, drug discovery, and advanced materials science.
• The government's National Quantum Mission is providing significant impetus to these indigenous efforts.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology
indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
prelimsPoints: Indian scientists achieve breakthrough in quantum computing
IISc and DRDO lab collaboration
Demonstrated stable 16-qubit quantum processor
Implications for secure communication, drug discovery, materials science
Supported by National Quantum Mission
mainsPoints: Discuss the fundamental principles of quantum computing and its potential to revolutionize various sectors.
Analyze the significance of India's indigenous breakthrough in quantum processor development for its technological sovereignty.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities in accelerating quantum technology research and development in India.
Examine the strategic importance of the National Quantum Mission in fostering a robust quantum ecosystem.
Suggest ethical considerations and policy frameworks required for responsible development and deployment of quantum technologies.
articles.imageDescription: Abstract image of quantum bits (qubits) connected in a network
articles.trivia: Unlike classical computers that use bits (0 or 1), quantum computers use qubits which can be 0, 1, or both simultaneously (superposition).

articles.headline: India Records Highest Ever Foreign Direct Investment Inflows in Q3 2024
articles.explainer:
• India witnessed its highest ever quarterly Foreign Direct Investment (FDI) inflows, reaching $XX billion in the third quarter of 2024.
• The surge is primarily driven by investments in manufacturing, digital services, and renewable energy sectors.
• Government's policy reforms, ease of doing business initiatives, and strong economic growth contributed to investor confidence.
• Key source countries for FDI included the USA, Singapore, and Mauritius, with significant interest from European investors.
• This record inflow underscores India's position as an attractive global investment destination despite global economic headwinds.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Investment models
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth
articles.source: Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT)
prelimsPoints: India records highest quarterly FDI inflows in Q3 2024
Driven by manufacturing, digital services, renewable energy
Attributed to policy reforms, ease of doing business, economic growth
Major source countries: USA, Singapore, Mauritius
Highlights India as attractive investment destination
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors contributing to the record Foreign Direct Investment inflows into India in the third quarter of 2024.
Discuss the significance of FDI in achieving India's economic growth targets and fostering technological advancements.
Evaluate the role of government policies, such as 'Make in India' and ease of doing business reforms, in attracting foreign capital.
Examine the sectoral distribution of FDI and its implications for India's industrial development and job creation.
Suggest further reforms and strategies to sustain and enhance FDI inflows amidst global economic uncertainties.
articles.imageDescription: Graph showing upward trend of FDI inflows into India
articles.trivia: Mauritius has historically been a significant source of FDI for India due to its favorable tax treaties, though its share has seen changes over time.

articles.headline: Health Ministry Launches National Mental Health Action Plan 2024-2030
articles.explainer:
• The Union Ministry of Health and Family Welfare launched the National Mental Health Action Plan (NMHAP) 2024-2030.
• The plan aims to integrate mental healthcare with primary healthcare, ensuring universal access to services.
• Key pillars include strengthening mental health infrastructure, capacity building of healthcare professionals, and destigmatization campaigns.
• It prioritizes early intervention for adolescents and youth, addressing the growing mental health burden in these age groups.
• The plan seeks to leverage digital platforms for tele-mental health services and expand community-based support.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes
articles.source: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
prelimsPoints: National Mental Health Action Plan (NMHAP) 2024-2030 launched
By Union Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
Aims for universal access, integration with primary healthcare
Pillars: infrastructure, capacity building, destigmatization
Prioritizes early intervention for youth, digital platforms
mainsPoints: Discuss the growing burden of mental health issues in India and the need for a comprehensive national action plan.
Analyze the key objectives and strategies of the National Mental Health Action Plan 2024-2030, particularly its focus on integration with primary healthcare.
Evaluate the challenges in implementing mental health initiatives in India, including stigma, lack of trained professionals, and funding gaps.
Examine the role of digital platforms and community-based approaches in improving access to mental healthcare services.
Suggest measures to foster a more supportive and inclusive environment for individuals with mental health conditions in India.
articles.imageDescription: Abstract image representing mental well-being and support
articles.trivia: The Mental Healthcare Act, 2017, decriminalized suicide in India and guaranteed every person the right to access mental healthcare.

articles.headline: IMD Predicts Above-Normal Winter Rainfall, Warns of Potential Floods in Southern States
articles.explainer:
• The India Meteorological Department (IMD) issued its winter outlook, predicting above-normal rainfall for parts of South and Central India during December-February.
• This forecast is influenced by persistent La Niña conditions and warmer than usual sea surface temperatures in the Indian Ocean.
• While beneficial for agriculture, the IMD warned of potential localized flooding, especially in low-lying coastal areas of Tamil Nadu, Andhra Pradesh, and Kerala.
• State disaster management authorities have been advised to take precautionary measures and prepare for increased rainfall events.
• The forecast highlights the increasing variability of weather patterns attributed to climate change.
gsSubTopics: Important Geophysical phenomena such as earthquakes, Tsunami, Volcanic activity, cyclone etc., geographical features and their location-changes in critical geographical features (including water-bodies and ice-caps) and in flora and fauna and the effects of such changes
Distribution of Key Natural Resources across the World (including South Asia and the Indian subcontinent)
prelimsPoints: IMD predicts above-normal winter rainfall for South and Central India
Forecast influenced by La Niña conditions
Warning of potential localized flooding in Tamil Nadu, Andhra Pradesh, Kerala
State disaster management authorities advised precautionary measures
Highlights climate change impact on weather patterns
mainsPoints: Discuss the factors influencing winter rainfall patterns in India, particularly the role of La Niña and local atmospheric conditions.
Analyze the potential socio-economic impacts of above-normal winter rainfall, including benefits for agriculture and risks of flooding.
Evaluate the preparedness of state disaster management authorities in Southern India to handle extreme weather events.
Examine how climate change is altering traditional weather patterns and increasing the frequency of extreme rainfall events.
Suggest long-term strategies for climate resilient infrastructure and effective early warning systems to mitigate flood risks.
articles.imageDescription: Satellite image showing cloud cover over Southern India
articles.trivia: La Niña is characterized by unusually cold ocean temperatures in the equatorial Pacific, compared to El Niño, which is characterized by unusually warm ocean temperatures.

articles.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Unearths Ancient Buddhist Monastery Remains in Odisha
articles.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) announced the discovery of the remains of an ancient Buddhist monastery in the Jajpur district of Odisha.
• The excavation revealed stupas, statues of Buddhist deities, and intricate carvings dating back to the 7th-8th century CE.
• This site adds to the rich Buddhist heritage of Odisha, particularly the 'Diamond Triangle' of Ratnagiri, Udayagiri, and Lalitgiri.
• The findings provide crucial insights into the spread of Buddhism in eastern India and the architectural styles of the period.
• Conservation and further exploration efforts are underway to preserve and study the newly discovered structures.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times
Ancient History
prelimsPoints: ASI discovers ancient Buddhist monastery remains in Jajpur, Odisha
Revealed stupas, Buddhist deity statues, carvings
Dates back to 7th-8th century CE
Adds to Odisha's 'Diamond Triangle' Buddhist heritage
Provides insights into Buddhism in eastern India
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of archaeological discoveries in reconstructing India's ancient history and cultural heritage.
Analyze the role of Odisha in the spread and development of Buddhism in eastern India, referencing the 'Diamond Triangle'.
Examine how such findings contribute to our understanding of ancient Indian art, architecture, and religious practices.
Evaluate the challenges and best practices in the conservation and preservation of archaeological sites in India.
Suggest ways to promote cultural tourism and public awareness about India's rich Buddhist heritage.
articles.imageDescription: Archaeological excavation site showing ancient brick structures
articles.trivia: The 'Diamond Triangle' of Odisha (Ratnagiri, Udayagiri, Lalitgiri) is known for its extensive Buddhist complexes, believed to be centers of learning and monastic life.

articles.headline: India's Tourism Sector Registers Record Growth, Exceeds Pre-Pandemic Levels
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Tourism announced that India's tourism sector has achieved record growth in 2024, surpassing pre-pandemic visitor numbers and revenue.
• Both domestic and international tourist arrivals showed robust increases, driven by government initiatives like 'Dekho Apna Desh' and improved connectivity.
• The hospitality sector, including hotels and homestays, reported high occupancy rates and significant investment.
• Focus on niche tourism segments like eco-tourism, adventure tourism, and spiritual circuits contributed to the growth.
• This resurgence is a major boost to employment generation and foreign exchange earnings.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: India's tourism sector records growth in 2024
Exceeds pre-pandemic visitor numbers and revenue
Driven by 'Dekho Apna Desh' and improved connectivity
High occupancy rates in hospitality sector
Focus on eco-tourism, adventure, spiritual circuits
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-faceted contributions of the tourism sector to India's economy, including employment generation and foreign exchange earnings.
Analyze the effectiveness of government initiatives and infrastructure development in boosting both domestic and international tourism.
Evaluate the potential of niche tourism segments like eco-tourism and spiritual tourism in promoting sustainable and inclusive growth.
Examine the challenges faced by the tourism sector, such as infrastructure deficits, environmental concerns, and skill gaps.
Suggest policy measures and marketing strategies to further enhance India's global tourism competitiveness and ensure sustainable development.
articles.imageDescription: Diverse Indian tourist destinations like Taj Mahal, beaches, and mountains
articles.trivia: The World Tourism Organization (UNWTO) defines tourism as 'a social, cultural and economic phenomenon which entails the movement of people to countries or places outside their usual environment for personal or business/professional purposes.'

articles.headline: India and Brazil Ink New Trade and Investment Cooperation Agreement
articles.explainer:
• India and Brazil signed a comprehensive Trade and Investment Cooperation Agreement (TICA) aimed at boosting bilateral trade and investment flows.
• The agreement focuses on reducing tariff and non-tariff barriers, promoting cross-border investments, and facilitating technology transfer.
• Key sectors targeted for enhanced cooperation include agriculture, pharmaceuticals, energy, and digital services.
• This move strengthens India's engagement with the MERCOSUR bloc and reinforces South-South cooperation.
• The TICA is expected to diversify trade baskets and create new opportunities for businesses in both countries.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India's interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India's interests
prelimsPoints: India and Brazil sign Trade and Investment Cooperation Agreement (TICA)
Aims to boost bilateral trade and investment
Focus on reducing barriers, promoting investments, technology transfer
Key sectors: agriculture, pharma, energy, digital services
Strengthens India's engagement with MERCOSUR
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of strengthening economic ties between India and Brazil, two major emerging economies.
Discuss the potential benefits of the Trade and Investment Cooperation Agreement (TICA) for diversifying India's trade and investment portfolio.
Evaluate the challenges in enhancing bilateral trade with Latin American countries like Brazil, including logistical and cultural barriers.
Examine the role of such agreements in promoting South-South cooperation and India's 'Look East' (or rather, 'Look West' in this context) policy.
Suggest measures to maximize the gains from this agreement and explore further avenues for India-Brazil collaboration.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and Brazil waving together
articles.trivia: Brazil is the largest country in South America and the fifth-largest country in the world by both area and population.

articles.headline: National Digital Health Mission Expands to Cover All Districts, Focus on EHR Integration
articles.explainer:
• The National Digital Health Mission (NDHM), now known as Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM), announced its expansion to cover all districts across India.
• The mission aims to create a seamless online platform for healthcare services, linking health records, doctors, and facilities.
• A key focus is on integrating Electronic Health Records (EHRs) across public and private healthcare providers to ensure interoperability.
• The expansion includes enhanced features for patient consent management, telemedicine consultations, and prescription management.
• This initiative is poised to revolutionize healthcare delivery, making it more accessible, efficient, and transparent.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
e-governance- applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential; citizens charters, transparency & accountability and institutional and other measures.
prelimsPoints: National Digital Health Mission (ABDM) expands to all districts
Aims for seamless online healthcare platform
Key focus: integrating Electronic Health Records (EHRs)
Enhanced features: consent management, telemedicine, prescription
Revolutionizes healthcare delivery for accessibility, efficiency, transparency
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative potential of the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) in addressing India's healthcare challenges.
Analyze the benefits of integrating Electronic Health Records (EHRs) and creating a unified digital health ecosystem.
Evaluate the challenges in implementing a nationwide digital health mission, including data privacy concerns, digital literacy, and infrastructure disparities.
Examine the role of telemedicine and digital platforms in improving healthcare access, especially in rural and remote areas.
Suggest policy measures to ensure data security, promote digital inclusion, and build trust in the digital health infrastructure.
articles.imageDescription: Smartphone screen showing digital health records interface
articles.trivia: The Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission was launched nationwide in September 2021, building upon the pilot project of the National Digital Health Mission.

highlights: Parliament passes landmark Urban Local Bodies Empowerment Bill, 2024, enhancing decentralised urban governance.
RBI maintains repo rate at 6.5% for the sixth consecutive time, emphasizing inflation control amidst global economic uncertainties.
India-EU Strategic Dialogue concludes, focusing on deepening cooperation in green transition, digital transformation, and trade.
ISRO successfully launches advanced Earth Observation Satellite EOS-07, bolstering India's remote sensing capabilities for national development.
Government unveils National Policy on Water Recycling and Reuse, 2024, targeting sustainable water management and enhanced water security.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the Urban Local Bodies Empowerment Bill, 2024, consider the following statements:
1. It proposes to grant greater financial autonomy to Urban Local Bodies (ULBs).
2. It mandates the establishment of Ward Committees only in metropolitan cities.
3. It introduces a performance-linked grant system for ULBs.

Which of the statements given above are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The Bill aims to provide greater financial autonomy to ULBs and direct devolution of funds. Statement 2 is incorrect: The Bill mandates the establishment of Ward Committees in *all* municipalities, not just metropolitan cities. Statement 3 is correct: A key provision of the Bill is a performance-linked grant system for ULBs.

prelimsMocks.question:
The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) of the RBI recently maintained the repo rate. Which of the following statements about the MPC are correct?
1. It consists of six members, with three from the RBI and three external members nominated by the Government of India.
2. The Governor of the RBI is ex-officio Chairperson of the MPC.
3. The primary objective of the MPC is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.

Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: All three statements are correct. The MPC indeed has six members (3 RBI, 3 government nominees). The RBI Governor is its ex-officio Chairperson. Its primary mandate is to maintain price stability, with a secondary objective of supporting growth.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding India's Earth Observation Satellites (EOS) program:
1. EOS-07, recently launched by ISRO, is designed to provide high-resolution imagery for applications like urban planning and disaster management.
2. The Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle (PSLV) is India's heaviest operational launch vehicle, primarily used for geostationary transfers.
3. India's first experimental satellite, Aryabhata, was launched indigenously by ISRO.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct. EOS-07 serves various applications including urban planning, agriculture, and disaster management. Statement 2 is incorrect. PSLV is a versatile vehicle for Earth Observation satellites, but GSLV is India's heavier launch vehicle for geostationary transfers. Statement 3 is incorrect. Aryabhata was launched by the Soviet Union in 1975, not indigenously by ISRO.

prelimsMocks.question:
The National Policy on Water Recycling and Reuse, 2024, launched by the Ministry of Jal Shakti, aims to:
1. Promote the use of treated wastewater for non-potable purposes.
2. Mandate the use of only potable water for all industrial applications.
3. Encourage public-private partnerships in wastewater treatment infrastructure.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct. The policy aims to increase the share of treated wastewater in non-potable uses. Statement 2 is incorrect. The policy would likely seek to reduce dependence on potable water for industrial uses by promoting recycled water. Statement 3 is correct. The policy encourages public-private partnerships for infrastructure development.

prelimsMocks.question:
Regarding the Anti-Defection Law in India, consider the following statements:
1. It was introduced through the 52nd Amendment Act to the Indian Constitution.
2. The law is enshrined in the Eighth Schedule of the Constitution.
3. A recent Parliamentary Panel recommended limiting the Speaker's discretionary powers in deciding disqualification petitions.

Which of the statements given above are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct. The Anti-Defection Law was added by the 52nd Amendment Act of 1985. Statement 2 is incorrect. The law is enshrined in the Tenth Schedule, not the Eighth Schedule (which deals with official languages). Statement 3 is correct. A recent parliamentary panel recommended limiting the Speaker's powers and setting time limits for decisions.

mainsMocks.question: Critically analyze the provisions of the Urban Local Bodies Empowerment Bill, 2024. How far do these provisions address the long-standing challenges of urban governance and financial autonomy for ULBs in India? (150 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the Bill's purpose. Discuss its key provisions like financial autonomy, participatory budgeting, Ward Committees. Evaluate how these strengthen 74th Amendment. Address potential challenges in implementation and whether it fully resolves issues like capacity building, resource generation, and accountability.

mainsMocks.question: The India-EU Strategic Dialogue emphasized green transition and trade. Discuss the strategic significance of this partnership for India amidst evolving global geopolitical dynamics and its implications for India's economic and environmental goals. (250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India's interests; Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India's interests.
mainsMocks.approach: Explain the growing importance of the India-EU partnership beyond traditional trade. Highlight areas of cooperation (green tech, digital, trade agreement). Analyze the geopolitical context (multipolar world, China factor, climate leadership). Discuss economic benefits (FTA, investment) and environmental implications (climate action, renewable energy). Conclude on its role in India's foreign policy objectives.

mainsMocks.question: In an era of increasingly sophisticated cyber threats, what are the key challenges facing India's cyber security landscape? Discuss how the proposed National Cyber Security Framework 2.0 aims to address these challenges and ensure the protection of India's critical information infrastructure. (250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Challenges to internal security through communication networks, basics of cyber security; role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges.
mainsMocks.approach: Begin by outlining the current cyber threat landscape (AI, IoT, quantum, state-sponsored attacks, ransomware). Detail the challenges for India (critical infrastructure protection, skill gap, legal framework, international coordination). Explain how NCSF 2.0 addresses these through pillars like critical infrastructure protection, cyber deterrence, capacity building, multi-stakeholder approach, and international cooperation. Conclude on the framework's potential impact and need for robust implementation.

quickBytes.text: The 74th Constitutional Amendment Act, 1992, gave constitutional status to Urban Local Bodies (ULBs).
The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is statutorily mandated to determine the policy interest rates required to achieve the inflation target.
Common But Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities (CBDR-RC) is a principle under UNFCCC.
PSLV (Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle) is ISRO's third-generation launch vehicle, known for its reliability in launching Earth Observation satellites.
The Prakash Singh case (2006) led to landmark Supreme Court directives for police reforms in India.
CERT-In (Indian Computer Emergency Response Team) is the national agency for cyber security incident response in India.
The National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 aims for universalization of education from pre-school to secondary level.
Production Linked Incentive (PLI) schemes are designed to boost domestic manufacturing and attract investment in key sectors.
The Snow Leopard (Panthera uncia) is listed as 'Vulnerable' on the IUCN Red List of Threatened Species.
La Niña is a climate pattern that describes the cooling of surface ocean waters along the tropical west coast of South America.

GS1.headline: IMD Predicts Above-Normal Winter Rainfall, Warns of Potential Floods in Southern States
GS1.explainer:
• The India Meteorological Department (IMD) issued its winter outlook, predicting above-normal rainfall for parts of South and Central India during December-February.
• This forecast is influenced by persistent La Niña conditions and warmer than usual sea surface temperatures in the Indian Ocean.
• While beneficial for agriculture, the IMD warned of potential localized flooding, especially in low-lying coastal areas of Tamil Nadu, Andhra Pradesh, and Kerala.
• State disaster management authorities have been advised to take precautionary measures and prepare for increased rainfall events.
• The forecast highlights the increasing variability of weather patterns attributed to climate change.
gsSubTopics: Important Geophysical phenomena such as earthquakes, Tsunami, Volcanic activity, cyclone etc., geographical features and their location-changes in critical geographical features (including water-bodies and ice-caps) and in flora and fauna and the effects of such changes
Distribution of Key Natural Resources across the World (including South Asia and the Indian subcontinent)
prelimsPoints: IMD predicts above-normal winter rainfall for South and Central India
Forecast influenced by La Niña conditions
Warning of potential localized flooding in Tamil Nadu, Andhra Pradesh, Kerala
State disaster management authorities advised precautionary measures
Highlights climate change impact on weather patterns
mainsPoints: Discuss the factors influencing winter rainfall patterns in India, particularly the role of La Niña and local atmospheric conditions.
Analyze the potential socio-economic impacts of above-normal winter rainfall, including benefits for agriculture and risks of flooding.
Evaluate the preparedness of state disaster management authorities in Southern India to handle extreme weather events.
Examine how climate change is altering traditional weather patterns and increasing the frequency of extreme rainfall events.
Suggest long-term strategies for climate resilient infrastructure and effective early warning systems to mitigate flood risks.
GS1.imageDescription: Satellite image showing cloud cover over Southern India
GS1.trivia: La Niña is characterized by unusually cold ocean temperatures in the equatorial Pacific, compared to El Niño, which is characterized by unusually warm ocean temperatures.

GS1.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Unearths Ancient Buddhist Monastery Remains in Odisha
GS1.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) announced the discovery of the remains of an ancient Buddhist monastery in the Jajpur district of Odisha.
• The excavation revealed stupas, statues of Buddhist deities, and intricate carvings dating back to the 7th-8th century CE.
• This site adds to the rich Buddhist heritage of Odisha, particularly the 'Diamond Triangle' of Ratnagiri, Udayagiri, and Lalitgiri.
• The findings provide crucial insights into the spread of Buddhism in eastern India and the architectural styles of the period.
• Conservation and further exploration efforts are underway to preserve and study the newly discovered structures.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times
Ancient History
prelimsPoints: ASI discovers ancient Buddhist monastery remains in Jajpur, Odisha
Revealed stupas, Buddhist deity statues, carvings
Dates back to 7th-8th century CE
Adds to Odisha's 'Diamond Triangle' Buddhist heritage
Provides insights into Buddhism in eastern India
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of archaeological discoveries in reconstructing India's ancient history and cultural heritage.
Analyze the role of Odisha in the spread and development of Buddhism in eastern India, referencing the 'Diamond Triangle'.
Examine how such findings contribute to our understanding of ancient Indian art, architecture, and religious practices.
Evaluate the challenges and best practices in the conservation and preservation of archaeological sites in India.
Suggest ways to promote cultural tourism and public awareness about India's rich Buddhist heritage.
GS1.imageDescription: Archaeological excavation site showing ancient brick structures
GS1.trivia: The 'Diamond Triangle' of Odisha (Ratnagiri, Udayagiri, Lalitgiri) is known for its extensive Buddhist complexes, believed to be centers of learning and monastic life.

GS2.headline: Parliament Passes Landmark Urban Local Bodies Empowerment Bill, 2024
GS2.explainer:
• The Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha passed the Urban Local Bodies Empowerment Bill, 2024, aiming to strengthen decentralised governance.
• The Bill proposes greater financial autonomy for ULBs, direct devolution of funds, and enhanced capacity building programs.
• It mandates the establishment of Ward Committees in all municipalities and introduces participatory budgeting mechanisms.
• Key provisions include a performance-linked grant system and a dedicated urban infrastructure development fund.
• The legislation is expected to significantly boost the effectiveness and accountability of urban local self-governments.
gsSubTopics: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States
issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure
devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein
prelimsPoints: Urban Local Bodies Empowerment Bill, 2024
Aims to strengthen 74th Constitutional Amendment Act provisions
Provisions for financial autonomy, direct fund devolution, capacity building
Mandates Ward Committees and participatory budgeting
Performance-linked grants and urban infrastructure fund
mainsPoints: Significance of the Bill in reinforcing democratic decentralisation in urban areas.
Challenges in implementation, including financial constraints and capacity deficits of ULBs.
Role of participatory budgeting and Ward Committees in enhancing citizen engagement and accountability.
Impact on urban planning, service delivery, and sustainable development goals.
Comparison with previous attempts at ULB reforms and lessons learned.
GS2.imageDescription: Indian Parliament building with 'Urban Local Bodies Empowerment Bill' text overlay
GS2.trivia: The 74th Constitutional Amendment Act, which gave constitutional status to Urban Local Bodies, was passed in 1992.

GS2.headline: India-EU Strategic Dialogue Concludes, Emphasizes Green Transition and Trade
GS2.explainer:
• The 18th India-EU Strategic Dialogue concluded in Brussels, focusing on deepening cooperation in green technologies, digital transformation, and trade.
• External Affairs Minister and EU High Representative for Foreign Affairs co-chaired the dialogue, reviewing progress on the India-EU Free Trade Agreement negotiations.
• Both sides committed to enhancing collaboration on climate action, renewable energy, and sustainable development goals.
• Discussions also covered geopolitical developments, regional security, and strengthening multilateralism.
• A joint statement highlighted the shared commitment to rules-based international order and resilient supply chains.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India's interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India's interests
prelimsPoints: 18th India-EU Strategic Dialogue in Brussels
Co-chaired by EAM and EU High Representative
Key focus areas: green technologies, digital transformation, trade
Progress reviewed on India-EU FTA negotiations
Commitment to climate action and sustainable development
mainsPoints: Analyze the growing strategic convergence between India and the EU, particularly in the context of global geopolitical shifts.
Discuss the potential economic benefits and challenges of the proposed India-EU Free Trade Agreement.
Examine the role of green transition and digital cooperation as pillars of the India-EU partnership.
Evaluate India's position and influence within multilateral forums through its engagement with the EU.
Suggest measures to further deepen the India-EU strategic partnership beyond economic and climate cooperation.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and European Union displayed together during a press conference
GS2.trivia: The European Union is India's third-largest trading partner, accounting for 10.8% of India's total trade in goods in 2022-23.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Directs States to Expedite Implementation of Police Reforms
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, hearing a Public Interest Litigation (PIL), issued fresh directives to all state governments and Union Territories for the expeditious implementation of police reforms.
• The court expressed concern over the slow progress in adopting recommendations from the Prakash Singh case and various expert committees.
• Directives include setting up State Security Commissions, ensuring fixed tenure for DGPs, and separating investigation from law and order functions.
• The bench emphasized the need for modernizing police infrastructure, improving training, and enhancing accountability mechanisms.
• This ruling reiterates the judiciary's push for a more professional, accountable, and citizen-friendly police force.
gsSubTopics: Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary
Ministries and Departments of the Government
pressure groups and formal/informal associations and their role in the Polity
Governance
transparency and accountability
keyTerms: Prakash Singh v. Union of India
Separation of Investigation and Law & Order
prelimsPoints: Supreme Court directs states on police reforms
References Prakash Singh case recommendations
Key directives: State Security Commissions, fixed DGP tenure, separation of investigation
Emphasizes modernization, training, accountability
Aims for professional and citizen-friendly police force
mainsPoints: Analyze the historical context and persistent challenges in implementing police reforms in India.
Discuss the key recommendations of the Prakash Singh case and their significance for police accountability and efficiency.
Evaluate the role of the judiciary in pushing for police reforms when the executive and legislature have shown slow progress.
Examine the potential impact of fixed tenures for DGPs and the separation of investigation functions on police professionalism.
Suggest comprehensive strategies, including legislative changes and public awareness, to achieve effective police reforms.
GS2.imageDescription: Supreme Court of India building facade
GS2.trivia: The 'Prakash Singh v. Union of India' judgment was delivered by the Supreme Court in 2006, outlining seven directives for police reforms.

GS2.headline: National Education Policy (NEP) Implementation Review Highlights Progress and Challenges
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education released a comprehensive review report on the implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
• The report noted significant progress in areas like foundational literacy and numeracy, curricular reforms, and integration of vocational education.
• Challenges identified include infrastructure gaps in remote areas, teacher training effectiveness, and equitable access to digital learning resources.
• The review emphasized the need for greater state-level coordination and increased budgetary allocation for education.
• Recommendations include accelerated teacher recruitment, strengthening digital infrastructure, and developing localized content.
gsSubTopics: Development processes and the development industry—the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
prelimsPoints: Ministry of Education releases NEP 2020 implementation review
Progress in foundational literacy, curricular reforms, vocational education
Challenges: infrastructure, teacher training, digital access
Recommendations: state coordination, budget increase, teacher recruitment
Focus on localized content development
mainsPoints: Analyze the transformative potential of the National Education Policy 2020 in reforming India's education system.
Discuss the key achievements in NEP implementation over the past few years, citing specific examples.
Examine the major hurdles and bottlenecks encountered during the policy's implementation, particularly in rural and underserved areas.
Suggest concrete policy interventions and resource allocation strategies to address the identified challenges and ensure equitable access to quality education.
Evaluate the long-term implications of NEP 2020 for India's human resource development and economic growth.
GS2.imageDescription: Students in a classroom setting with digital learning tools
GS2.trivia: The National Education Policy 2020 is the third major education policy in India since independence, following those in 1968 and 1986.

GS2.headline: India Advocates for Global South Concerns at UN Climate Conference Prep Meet
GS2.explainer:
• India actively participated in the preparatory meeting for the upcoming UN Climate Change Conference (COP29), advocating for the concerns of the Global South.
• The Indian delegation emphasized the principles of Common But Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities (CBDR-RC).
• Key demands included increased climate finance from developed nations, technology transfer, and support for adaptation measures in developing countries.
• India highlighted the need for a balanced approach to climate action, ensuring energy security and sustainable development for all nations.
• The discussions set the stage for contentious negotiations on mitigation, adaptation, and loss and damage at COP29.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India's interests
GS2.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
keyTerms: Common But Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities (CBDR-RC)
prelimsPoints: India at UN Climate Conference (COP29) prep meet
Advocated for Global South concerns
Emphasized CBDR-RC principle
Demands: increased climate finance, technology transfer, adaptation support
Called for balanced approach to climate action
mainsPoints: Analyze India's leadership role in representing the interests of the Global South in international climate negotiations.
Discuss the significance of the principle of 'Common But Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities' (CBDR-RC) in achieving climate justice.
Evaluate the challenges in mobilizing adequate climate finance and technology transfer from developed to developing countries.
Examine the implications of climate change on India's development trajectory and its efforts towards energy transition.
Suggest strategies for India to strengthen its position and achieve favorable outcomes for the Global South at COP29.
GS2.imageDescription: World map with countries highlighted for climate change discussions
GS2.trivia: The first Conference of the Parties (COP) to the UNFCCC was held in Berlin, Germany, in 1995.

GS2.headline: Parliamentary Panel Recommends Reforms in Anti-Defection Law to Ensure Stability
GS2.explainer:
• A Joint Parliamentary Committee (JPC) submitted its report recommending significant reforms to the Anti-Defection Law (Tenth Schedule of the Constitution).
• The report suggests clarifying the definition of 'defection' and limiting the Speaker's discretionary powers in deciding disqualification petitions.
• Recommendations include setting a strict time limit for the Speaker to decide on defection cases and exploring the role of the Election Commission.
• The panel aimed to prevent frequent defections leading to government instability while safeguarding genuine dissent.
• The proposed changes seek to strike a balance between party discipline and the democratic rights of legislators.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these
prelimsPoints: JPC recommends reforms to Anti-Defection Law (Tenth Schedule)
Suggestions: clarify defection, limit Speaker's powers
Strict time limit for Speaker's decision proposed
Role of Election Commission to be explored
Aims to prevent instability and protect dissent
mainsPoints: Discuss the original intent and evolution of the Anti-Defection Law in India and its impact on political stability.
Analyze the criticisms leveled against the current Anti-Defection Law, particularly regarding the Speaker's role and the ambiguity of 'defection'.
Evaluate the recommendations of the Parliamentary Panel, such as limiting the Speaker's powers and setting time limits, for their potential effectiveness.
Examine the implications of these reforms for party discipline, legislative integrity, and the democratic rights of elected representatives.
Suggest comprehensive reforms to the Anti-Defection Law that can balance political stability with democratic principles.
GS2.imageDescription: Indian Parliament building with scales of justice overlay
GS2.trivia: The Anti-Defection Law was added to the Indian Constitution as the Tenth Schedule by the 52nd Amendment Act of 1985.

GS2.headline: Health Ministry Launches National Mental Health Action Plan 2024-2030
GS2.explainer:
• The Union Ministry of Health and Family Welfare launched the National Mental Health Action Plan (NMHAP) 2024-2030.
• The plan aims to integrate mental healthcare with primary healthcare, ensuring universal access to services.
• Key pillars include strengthening mental health infrastructure, capacity building of healthcare professionals, and destigmatization campaigns.
• It prioritizes early intervention for adolescents and youth, addressing the growing mental health burden in these age groups.
• The plan seeks to leverage digital platforms for tele-mental health services and expand community-based support.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes
GS2.source: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare

mainsPoints: Discuss the growing burden of mental health issues in India and the need for a comprehensive national action plan.

Evaluate the challenges in implementing mental health initiatives in India, including stigma, lack of trained professionals, and funding gaps.
Examine the role of digital platforms and community-based approaches in improving access to mental healthcare services.
Suggest measures to foster a more supportive and inclusive environment for individuals with mental health conditions in India.
GS2.imageDescription: Abstract image representing mental well-being and support
GS2.trivia: The Mental Healthcare Act, 2017, decriminalized suicide in India and guaranteed every person the right to access mental healthcare.

• This move strengthens India's engagement with the MERCOSUR bloc and reinforces South-South cooperation.
